name: meep-photonic-devices-advanced-v1
description: Higher-budget photonic-device benchmark with literature-shaped metrics, coarse-to-fine search, and a third optional scatterer block.
paper_hypothesis: A coarse-to-fine Bayesian search over a slightly more expressive photonic geometry family should improve literature-shaped device metrics relative to the current refined benchmark.

baseline_notes: Treat these as block-based photonic design improvements, not topology optimization.
Judge the result by insertion loss, isolation or crosstalk, and bandwidth-style metrics, not just the aggregate score.
The coarse-to-fine optimizer should first explore globally, then narrow its search around the strongest observed region.
